overview: Gayatri Vidya Parishad College of Engineering for Women (GVPCEW), established in 2008, is a premier self-financed engineering institution in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. Dedicated to the empowerment of women through technical education, the college is situated in a secure, serene valley in Madhurawada. It is recognized for its disciplined academic culture, strong industry-oriented training, and high safety standards for its residential and day-scholar students.
accreditation: Accredited by NAAC with an 'A' Grade. Eligible B.Tech programs (CSE, ECE, IT) are accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA). Approved by AICTE, New Delhi, and affiliated with Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Kakinada (JNTUK).

coursesAndFees.course: B.Tech (CSE, AI&ML, Cyber Security, ECE, EEE, IT)
coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 50% aggregate in PCM + AP EAPCET rank

coursesAndFees.course: M.Tech (Data Science, VLSI Design & Embedded Systems)
coursesAndFees.eligibility: B.E./B.Tech in relevant branch + GATE / AP PGECET score

placements.highestSalary: ₹23.0 LPA - ₹35.0 LPA (Top packages typically from Wayfair, Adobe, and Amazon)

admissions.admissionProcess: Admissions are strictly governed by APSCHE guidelines. 70% of seats (Category A) are filled via state-level web counseling based on AP EAPCET ranks. 30% are Management Quota (Category B) seats filled based on merit. Counseling Code: GVPW.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026: May 12–18, 2026; Results: June 1, 2026; B.Tech Counseling: Tentatively July 2026; AP PGECET 2026 Results: May 14, 2026.

facilities: Central Library with Digital Resource Centre
Secure On-campus Girls Hostels with 24/7 Security
Transport Facility (Fleet covering all major points in Visakhapatnam)
campus.specialNote: GVPCEW is highly regarded for its safety and security protocols, specifically tailored for women. The institution encourages research through specialized faculty-led projects and provides extensive Training & Placement (T&P) assistance, including coding bootcamps and mock interview sessions from the third year onwards.
